# Feature skills

This directory holds feature-based context files generated by
[Skill Generator v2](https://github.com/bipinhcs11/Skill_Generator). It is
the durable source of truth that AI assistants (Copilot, Claude, Codex)
read before answering feature questions or editing code.

---

## What lives here

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `catalog.md` | Discovery layer — maps natural-language phrases to skill IDs |
| `<feature-id>/SKILL.md` | One file per feature; full feature context |
| `.skill-gen-audit.md` | Audit log of the most recent generation run |
| `.skill-update-audit.md` | Audit log of update runs (append-only) |
| `.archive/` | Deprecated/archived skills (read-only history) |

---

## How developers use this directory

You usually do not read these files yourself. Your AI assistant does.

When you start a conversation with Copilot Chat, Claude, or Codex about a
feature in this repo, the assistant should:

1. Look up your phrasing in `catalog.md`
2. Read the matching `SKILL.md` (and its `depends_on` chain)
3. Use that context to answer or to plan code changes

If the assistant is not doing this, check that the per-host instruction
file is in place:

- `.github/copilot-instructions.md` (for GitHub Copilot Chat)
- `CLAUDE.md` (at repo root, for Claude Code)
- `AGENTS.md` (at repo root, for Codex and generic agents)

These tell each host AI to read this directory first.

---

## How to refresh skills after a code change

When you change Java, properties/YAML, MyBatis mapper XML, SQL/migrations,
Spring Batch jobs, or operational scripts that affect feature behavior:

1. Open the host AI session in this repo
2. Trigger `skill-tracker` ("which skills are impacted by this change?")
3. Review the tracker report
4. If updates are needed, trigger `skill-updater` ("update feature skills")
5. Review the proposed edits at the updater's halt gate
6. Approve commit

The tracker is read-only. The updater only edits skills you approved at
the halt gate. Neither agent edits your application code.

---

## Confidence and review_required

Each skill carries `confidence` (HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW) and `review_required`
(true / false) in its frontmatter. Treat LOW-confidence or
`review_required: true` skills as drafts: the AI used them but flagged
uncertainty. A feature lead should review them when possible.

---

## How to add a new feature skill

If you add a new feature and no existing skill covers it:

1. Trigger `skill-generator` and tell it to generate just the new feature
2. Or trigger `skill-tracker` first; it will flag the missing skill and
   suggest the next step

Do not write `SKILL.md` files by hand — the generator's evidence phase
and halt gates catch defects that hand-written skills miss.

---

## Don't edit this directory by hand

The skills here are auto-generated by Skill Generator v2. Hand edits get
overwritten by the next `skill-updater` run. If a skill is wrong:

- For factual errors: run `skill-tracker` then `skill-updater`
- For metadata changes (aliases, business_terms, owner_team): edit the
  frontmatter only, then run `skill-updater` to regenerate the catalog

The `.skill-gen-audit.md` and `.skill-update-audit.md` files are the
permanent record of why each skill looks the way it does — preserve them.
